ip_outcome_note: Card name matches. PokemonTCG.io confirms swsh3-154 is Rookidee, National Pokedex no. 821. Art — small blue-black bird with pale underbelly and head crest — matches Rookidee's canonical design. Verification unambiguous.

trivia_bullets.text: Rookidee is National Pokedex no. 821, a pure Flying-type introduced in Generation VIII (Sword & Shield, 2019), classified as the Tiny Bird Pokemon. It evolves into Corvisquire at level 18, then into Corviknight at level 38. Corviknight, the final form, operates as Galar's aerial taxi service — a civilian utility role unique among Pokemon final evolutions. [PokemonTCG.io API: swsh3-154; WebSearch: Bulbapedia/Rookidee; PokemonDB]

Flavor text on this card: 'It will bravely challenge any opponent, no matter how powerful. This Pokemon benefits from every battle — even a defeat increases its strength a bit.' The detail about gaining strength from defeat is mechanically reflected in the card's Pluck attack, which removes Tools before dealing damage. [PokemonTCG.io API: swsh3-154]

Rookidee's design appears to draw inspiration from birds in the Paridae family (chickadees and tits) rather than directly from corvids, though it shares the same Passeriformes order as its corvid-inspired evolutions Corvisquire and Corviknight. Illustrated by Hitoshi Ariga, a manga artist known for the Mega Man Megamix series who also does substantial Pokemon TCG illustration work. [WebSearch: Bulbapedia/Rookidee design; PokemonTCG.io API]
